questions.question: What is the Python Virtual Machine (PVM)?
choices: A separate application you must install
The runtime engine that executes Python byte code
A virtual operating system for Python
A debugging tool for Python programs
questions.explanation: The PVM is the runtime engine of Python. It's the component that actually executes your compiled byte code. It's not a separate program — it's part of the Python system.

questions.question: Python source files use the ___ file extension.
questions.explanation: Python source code files conventionally use the .py extension. Compiled byte code files use .pyc.

questions.question: What happens when you import a Python module for the first time?
choices: The source code is executed directly without compilation
Python compiles the source to byte code and then executes it
Python converts it to C and compiles that
The module is loaded from a pre-compiled library
questions.explanation: When a module is first imported, Python compiles the source (.py) to byte code (.pyc) and saves it. On subsequent imports, Python loads the byte code directly if the source hasn't changed.

questions.question: Compiled Python byte code files have the ___ extension and are stored in the __pycache__ directory.
questions.explanation: Python 3 stores compiled byte code in __pycache__ directories as .pyc files, named with the Python version for compatibility.

questions.question: Which Python implementation uses a Just-In-Time (JIT) compiler to speed up execution?
questions.explanation: PyPy is an alternative Python implementation that includes a JIT compiler, which can significantly speed up long-running programs by compiling frequently-used byte code to machine code at runtime.

questions.question: What is 'frozen binaries' in the context of Python?
choices: Python code that runs only in cold weather
Standalone executables that bundle Python byte code with the PVM
Python code that has been encrypted
questions.explanation: Frozen binaries bundle compiled Python byte code with the PVM into a single executable package that end users can run without installing Python. Tools like PyInstaller and cx_Freeze create frozen binaries.
